member.name: T:Havit.Blazor.Components.Web.Bootstrap.HxAccordion
member.summary:
Bootstrap accordion component (built on the native <details>/<summary> disclosure elements, no JavaScript needed).
Full documentation and demos: https://havit.blazor.eu/components/HxAccordion

member.name: P:Havit.Blazor.Components.Web.Bootstrap.HxAccordion.CssClass
member.summary: Additional CSS classes for the accordion container.

member.name: P:Havit.Blazor.Components.Web.Bootstrap.HxAccordion.Color
member.summary: Theme color of the accordion (renders the theme-* class applied to open items).

member.name: P:Havit.Blazor.Components.Web.Bootstrap.HxAccordion.ExpandedItemIds
member.summary: IDs of the expanded items (if is true, several items can be expanded). Do not use a constant value as it resets the accordion on every roundtrip. Use to set the initial state. When is true, this parameter must be used to manage expanded items, and the event callback will be invoked for changes.

member.name: P:Havit.Blazor.Components.Web.Bootstrap.HxAccordion.ExpandedItemId
member.summary: ID of the single expanded item. If is true, this parameter is ignored, and you must use instead. When is true, the event callback will not be invoked. Do not use a constant value as it reverts the accordion to that item on every roundtrip. Use to set the initial state.

member.name: P:Havit.Blazor.Components.Web.Bootstrap.HxAccordion.StayOpen
member.summary: Set to true to make accordion items stay open when another item is opened. The default is false, opening another item collapses the current item. When true, must be used to manage expanded items.

member.name: P:Havit.Blazor.Components.Web.Bootstrap.HxAccordion.InitialExpandedItemId
member.summary: ID of the item you want to expand at the very beginning (overwrites if set).

member.name: P:Havit.Blazor.Components.Web.Bootstrap.HxAccordion.InitialExpandedItemIds
member.summary: IDs of the items you want to expand at the very beginning (overwrites if set).

member.name: M:Havit.Blazor.Components.Web.Bootstrap.HxAccordion.NotifyItemExpandedAsync(Havit.Blazor.Components.Web.Bootstrap.HxAccordionItem)
member.summary: Called by an expanding . For exclusive accordions ( is false), collapses all other expanded items first (replicates the native name-attribute exclusivity Blazor-side, as the open state is managed by Blazor).

member.name: T:Havit.Blazor.Components.Web.Bootstrap.HxAccordionItem
member.summary: Single item for .

member.summary: Clickable header (always visible).

member.summary: Body (collapsible).

member.name: P:Havit.Blazor.Components.Web.Bootstrap.HxAccordionItem.Id
member.summary: ID of the item (). (Gets generated GUID if not set.)

member.summary: Raised when the item is expanded. Is not raised for the initial rendering even if the item is expanded.

member.summary: Triggers the event. Allows interception of the event in derived components.

member.summary: Raised when the item is collapsed. Is not raised for the initial rendering even if the item is collapsed.

member.summary: Triggers the event. Allows interception of the event in derived components.

member.summary: Additional CSS class(es) for the accordion item.

member.summary: Additional CSS class(es) for the accordion item header (.accordion-header).

member.name: T:Havit.Blazor.Components.Web.Bootstrap.HxAvatarStack
member.summary:
Bootstrap Avatar stack component. Groups multiple components with an overlapping effect. Avatars are rendered in reverse order, so the first avatar appears on top. To show a count of additional users, add an initials avatar (e.g. <HxAvatar Color="ThemeColor.Secondary">+5</HxAvatar>) as the last item.
Full documentation and demos: https://havit.blazor.eu/components/HxAvatarStack

member.name: M:Havit.Blazor.Components.Web.Bootstrap.ButtonVariantExtensions.ToButtonVariantAndColorCssClass(Havit.Blazor.Components.Web.Bootstrap.ButtonVariant,Havit.Blazor.Components.Web.Bootstrap.ThemeColor)
member.summary: Bootstrap 6 buttons compose a variant class (btn-solid, btn-outline, ...) with a theme color class (theme-*). Without a color, the variant classes have no theme tokens to consume, so no variant class is emitted (a plain neutral btn look), with the exception of the standalone btn-link variant.

member.name: T:Havit.Blazor.Components.Web.Bootstrap.HxCloseButton
member.summary:
Bootstrap close-button component.
A simple close button for dismissing content such as dialogs and alerts. The icon is rendered with a CSS mask-image tinted with currentcolor, so it adapts to the surrounding text color and color mode automatically (the former White parameter/btn-close-white class no longer exist; use data-bs-theme="dark" on the button or a parent element to invert it explicitly).
Full documentation and demos: https://havit.blazor.eu/components/HxCloseButton

member.name: M:Havit.Blazor.Components.Web.Bootstrap.HxDialog.HandleCloseButtonClick
member.summary: Closes the dialog.

Closes the dialog from the header close button. The button also carries data-bs-dismiss="dialog" for the Bootstrap data API (static SSR); in interactive render modes this explicit handler closes the dialog through the component (both paths are idempotent - the plugin's hide() no-ops on a closed dialog).

member.summary: No backdrop will be rendered. Users can interact with other parts of the app while the drawer is open.

member.name: F:Havit.Blazor.Components.Web.Bootstrap.DrawerRenderMode.OpenOnly
member.summary: Drawer content is rendered only when it is open. Suitable for item-detail, item-edit, etc. This setting applies only when is set. For all other values, the content is always rendered (to be available for the mobile version).

member.name: F:Havit.Blazor.Components.Web.Bootstrap.DrawerRenderMode.Always
member.summary: Drawer content is always rendered (and hidden with CSS if not open). Needed for HxFilterForm with HxChipList.

member.name: T:Havit.Blazor.Components.Web.Bootstrap.DrawerResponsiveBreakpoint
member.summary: Content is displayed using the drawer when below this breakpoint, otherwise, contents are rendered in-place.

member.name: F:Havit.Blazor.Components.Web.Bootstrap.DrawerResponsiveBreakpoint.None
member.summary: Always render contents outside the viewport in an drawer.

member.name: F:Havit.Blazor.Components.Web.Bootstrap.DrawerResponsiveBreakpoint.Small
member.summary: Render contents outside the viewport in an drawer when below the "small" breakpoint (576px, exclusive).

member.name: F:Havit.Blazor.Components.Web.Bootstrap.DrawerResponsiveBreakpoint.Medium
member.summary: Render contents outside the viewport in an drawer when below the "medium" breakpoint (768px, exclusive).

member.name: F:Havit.Blazor.Components.Web.Bootstrap.DrawerResponsiveBreakpoint.Large
member.summary: Render contents outside the viewport in an drawer when below the "large" breakpoint (1024px, exclusive).

member.name: F:Havit.Blazor.Components.Web.Bootstrap.DrawerResponsiveBreakpoint.ExtraLarge
member.summary: Render contents outside the viewport in an drawer when below the "extra-large" breakpoint (1280px, exclusive).

member.name: F:Havit.Blazor.Components.Web.Bootstrap.DrawerResponsiveBreakpoint.Xxl
member.summary: Render contents outside the viewport in an drawer when below the "2xl" breakpoint (1536px, exclusive).

member.name: P:Havit.Blazor.Components.Web.Bootstrap.HxDrawer.OnShown
member.summary: This event is fired when an drawer element has been made visible to the user (will wait for CSS transitions to complete).

member.summary: Triggers the event. Allows interception of the event in derived components.

member.name: P:Havit.Blazor.Components.Web.Bootstrap.HxDrawer.OnHiding
member.summary: Fired immediately when the 'hide' instance method is called. To cancel hiding, set to true.
member.remarks: 1) This event should probably be named OnClosing to be consistent with other members, but "hide" is the Bootstrap event name and we should stick to it. We should consider renaming the other members in the future. 2) There is intentionally no virtual InvokeOnHidingAsync() method to override to avoid confusion. The hide.bs.drawer event is only subscribed to when the callback is set.

member.name: T:Havit.Blazor.Components.Web.Bootstrap.Internal.ChipRemoveActionBuilder
member.summary:
ChipRemoveActionBuilder is responsible for generating an Action that resets a property referenced by a lambda expression to a specified value (typically default).
Unlike most expression-based tools, this builder defers all expression analysis to the point of action invocation. This enables fast action construction (suitable for per-render use) and evaluates constraints and assumptions only when the action is actually executed.

member.name: M:Havit.Blazor.Components.Web.Bootstrap.Internal.MarkdownInlineParser.InlineCodeRegex
member.remarks:
Pattern:
`([^`]+)`
Explanation:
○ Match '`'.
○ 1st capture group.
○ Match a character other than '`' atomically at least once.
○ Match '`'.

member.name: M:Havit.Blazor.Components.Web.Bootstrap.Internal.MarkdownInlineParser.ImageRegex
member.remarks:
Pattern:
!\\[([^\\]]*)\\]\\(([^\\s\\)]+)(?:\\s+"([^"]*)")?\\)
Explanation:
○ Match the string "![".
○ 1st capture group.
○ Match a character other than ']' atomically any number of times.
○ Match the string "](".
○ 2nd capture group.
○ Match a character in the set [^)\s] greedily at least once.
○ Optional (greedy).
○ Match a whitespace character atomically at least once.
○ Match '"'.
○ 3rd capture group.
○ Match a character other than '"' atomically any number of times.
○ Match '"'.
○ Match ')'.

member.name: M:Havit.Blazor.Components.Web.Bootstrap.Internal.MarkdownInlineParser.LinkRegex
member.remarks:
Pattern:
\\[([^\\]]+)\\]\\(([^\\s\\)]+)(?:\\s+"([^"]*)")?\\)
Explanation:
○ Match '['.
○ 1st capture group.
○ Match a character other than ']' atomically at least once.
○ Match the string "](".
○ 2nd capture group.
○ Match a character in the set [^)\s] greedily at least once.
○ Optional (greedy).
○ Match a whitespace character atomically at least once.
○ Match '"'.

member.name: P:Havit.Blazor.Components.Web.Bootstrap.HxCheckboxBase.NeedsInnerDiv
member.summary: For a checkbox without Label/LabelTemplate, the form-field layout class goes to the parent div (allows combining with CssClass etc.). For a checkbox with Label, there is a parent div followed by a label for Label/LabelTemplate, so the input and text-label are wrapped in an additional div.form-field.

member.name: T:Havit.Blazor.Components.Web.Bootstrap.HxInputNumber`1
member.summary:
Numeric input.
Full documentation and demos: https://havit.blazor.eu/components/HxInputNumber
member.remarks: Defaults located in separate non-generic type .
member.typeparam: Supported values: byte (Byte), sbyte (SByte), short (Int16), ushort(UInt16), int (Int32), uint(UInt32), long (Int64), ulong(UInt64), float (Single), double (Double) and decimal (Decimal).

member.name: M:Havit.Blazor.Components.Web.Bootstrap.HxInputNumber`1.GetDefaults
member.summary: Returns application-wide defaults for the component. Enables overriding defaults in descendants (use separate set of defaults).

member.summary: Set of settings to be applied to the component instance (overrides , overridden by individual parameters).

member.name: M:Havit.Blazor.Components.Web.Bootstrap.HxInputNumber`1.GetSettings
member.summary: Returns optional set of component settings.
member.remarks: Similar to , enables defining wider in components descendants (by returning a derived settings class).

member.name: P:Havit.Blazor.Components.Web.Bootstrap.HxInputNumber`1.ParsingErrorMessage
member.summary: Gets or sets the error message used when displaying an a parsing error. Used with String.Format(...), {0} is replaced by Label property, {1} name of bounded property.

member.name: P:Havit.Blazor.Components.Web.Bootstrap.HxInputNumber`1.InputMode
member.summary:
Hint to browsers as to the type of virtual keyboard configuration to use when editing.
If not set (neither with nor , i.e. null), the will be used for equal to 0.
member.remarks:
We cannot set for greater that 0 as the users with keyboard locale not matching the application locale won't be able to enter decimal point (is , in some locales and . in others).
Feel free to set the InputMode on your own as needed.

member.name: P:Havit.Blazor.Components.Web.Bootstrap.HxInputNumber`1.Type
member.summary: Allows switching between textual and numeric input types. Only (default) and are supported.

member.name: P:Havit.Blazor.Components.Web.Bootstrap.HxInputNumber`1.Placeholder
member.summary: Placeholder for the input.

member.name: P:Havit.Blazor.Components.Web.Bootstrap.HxInputNumber`1.InputSize
member.summary: Size of the input.

member.name: P:Havit.Blazor.Components.Web.Bootstrap.HxInputNumber`1.BindEvent
member.summary: Input event used to bind the Value. Default is OnChange.

member.name: P:Havit.Blazor.Components.Web.Bootstrap.HxInputNumber`1.SelectOnFocus
member.summary: Determines whether all the content within the input field is automatically selected when it receives focus.

member.name: P:Havit.Blazor.Components.Web.Bootstrap.HxInputNumber`1.SmartPaste
member.summary: When enabled, pasted values are normalized to contain only valid numeric characters. Default is true.

member.name: P:Havit.Blazor.Components.Web.Bootstrap.HxInputNumber`1.SmartKeyboard
member.summary: When enabled, the input may provide an optimized keyboard experience for numeric entry. Currently, this means whenever a minus key is pressed, the sign of the number is toggled. Default is true.

member.summary: Gets or sets the number of decimal digits. Can be used only for floating point types, for integer types throws exception (for values other than 0). When not set, 2 decimal digits are used.

member.name: T:Havit.Blazor.Components.Web.Bootstrap.HxOtpInput
member.summary:
Bootstrap OTP input component (new in Bootstrap 6).
Connected input fields for one-time passwords, PIN codes, and verification codes with automatic focus advancement, backspace navigation, paste support, and browser autofill (autocomplete="one-time-code"). The Value is bound as a (the concatenated code).
Full documentation and demos: https://havit.blazor.eu/components/HxOtpInput

member.summary: Set of settings to be applied to the component instance (overrides , overridden by individual parameters).

member.summary: Returns an optional set of component settings.
member.remarks: Similar to , enables defining wider in component descendants (by returning a derived settings class).

member.name: P:Havit.Blazor.Components.Web.Bootstrap.HxOtpInput.Length
member.summary: The number of inputs (digits) to render. The default is 6.

member.name: P:Havit.Blazor.Components.Web.Bootstrap.HxOtpInput.Mask
member.summary: When true, the inputs render as type="password" fields to hide the entered values. The default is false.

member.name: P:Havit.Blazor.Components.Web.Bootstrap.HxOtpInput.Connected
member.summary: When true, the inputs are visually connected into a single cohesive field (Bootstrap input-group styles). When used together with , each group of inputs is wrapped in a nested input-group. The default is false.

member.name: P:Havit.Blazor.Components.Web.Bootstrap.HxOtpInput.GroupSize
member.summary: When set, the inputs are split into groups of this size with a visual between the groups (e.g. 123-456). The separator is purely visual, it does not affect the Value. The default is null (no groups).

member.name: P:Havit.Blazor.Components.Web.Bootstrap.HxOtpInput.Separator
member.summary: The separator text rendered between the groups of inputs (). The default is – (en dash).

member.name: P:Havit.Blazor.Components.Web.Bootstrap.HxOtpInput.OnCompleted
member.summary: Raised when all inputs are filled (Bootstrap complete.bs.otp-input event). Receives the complete code as a parameter.

member.name: M:Havit.Blazor.Components.Web.Bootstrap.HxOtpInput.InvokeOnCompletedAsync(System.String)
member.summary: Triggers the event. Allows interception of the event in derived components.

member.name: M:Havit.Blazor.Components.Web.Bootstrap.HxOtpInput.BuildRenderInput_RenderDigitInput(Microsoft.AspNetCore.Components.Rendering.RenderTreeBuilder,System.Int32)
member.summary: Renders a single digit input. The attributes follow the Bootstrap OTP input accessibility guidance (the same attributes the Bootstrap plugin sets on the inputs, rendered by Blazor to keep the render tree consistent with the DOM).

member.name: M:Havit.Blazor.Components.Web.Bootstrap.HxOtpInput.HandleJsInput(System.String)
member.summary: Receives notification from JavaScript when the value of any of the inputs changes (Bootstrap input.bs.otp-input event).
member.remarks: The method is intended for JS interop, do not call it directly.

member.name: M:Havit.Blazor.Components.Web.Bootstrap.HxOtpInput.HandleJsComplete(System.String)
member.summary: Receives notification from JavaScript when all inputs are filled (Bootstrap complete.bs.otp-input event).
member.remarks: The method is intended for JS interop, do not call it directly.

member.name: M:Havit.Blazor.Components.Web.Bootstrap.HxOtpInput.FocusAsync
member.summary: Focuses the component (the first empty input, or the last input when all inputs are filled).

member.name: M:Havit.Blazor.Components.Web.Bootstrap.Forms.Internal.DateHelper.GetRegex_DayMonthYear_Strict
member.remarks:
Pattern:
^(?<day>\\d{2})(?<month>\\d{2})(?<year>\\d{2}|\\d{4})$
Explanation:
○ Match if at the beginning of the string.
○ "day" capture group.
○ Match a Unicode digit exactly 2 times.
○ "month" capture group.
○ Match a Unicode digit exactly 2 times.
○ "year" capture group.
○ Match with 2 alternative expressions.
○ Match a Unicode digit exactly 2 times.
○ Match a Unicode digit exactly 4 times.
○ Match if at the end of the string or if before an ending newline.

member.name: M:Havit.Blazor.Components.Web.Bootstrap.Forms.Internal.DateHelper.GetRegex_DayMonthYear_Relaxed
member.remarks:
Pattern:
^\\W*(?<day>\\d{1,2})\\W+(?<month>\\d{1,2})\\W+(?<year>\\d{2}|\\d{4})\\W*$
Explanation:
○ Match if at the beginning of the string.
○ Match any character other than a word character greedily any number of times.
○ "day" capture group.
○ Match a Unicode digit greedily at least 1 and at most 2 times.
○ Match any character other than a word character greedily at least once.
○ "month" capture group.
○ Match a Unicode digit greedily at least 1 and at most 2 times.
○ Match any character other than a word character greedily at least once.
○ "year" capture group.
○ Match with 2 alternative expressions.
○ Match a Unicode digit exactly 2 times.
○ Match a Unicode digit exactly 4 times.
○ Match any character other than a word character greedily any number of times.
○ Match if at the end of the string or if before an ending newline.

member.name: T:Havit.Blazor.Components.Web.Bootstrap.HxPasswordStrength
member.summary:
Bootstrap Password strength meter (new in Bootstrap 6).
Provides real-time visual feedback on password strength. Attaches the Bootstrap Strength plugin to a password input—either a password input placed in or any input targeted with . The password is evaluated in JavaScript on every keystroke (no server round-trips, the password itself never leaves the browser); strength level changes are surfaced to .NET via .
This is not a form input component—it does not participate in EditForm validation and does not interfere with validation classes of the associated input.
Full documentation and demos: https://havit.blazor.eu/components/HxPasswordStrength

member.summary: Number of segments of the segmented meter (corresponds to the number of strength levels).

member.summary: Application-wide defaults for and derived components.

member.summary: Returns application-wide defaults for the component. Enables overriding defaults in descendants (use a separate set of defaults).

member.summary: Set of settings to be applied to the component instance (overrides , overridden by individual parameters).

member.summary: Returns an optional set of component settings.
member.remarks: Similar to , enables defining wider in component descendants (by returning a derived settings class).

member.name: P:Havit.Blazor.Components.Web.Bootstrap.HxPasswordStrength.ChildContent
member.summary: Content rendered above the strength meter, typically containing the password input the meter should evaluate (e.g. HxInputText with Type="InputType.Password"). When no is set, the meter automatically attaches to the first input[type="password"] found in the content.

member.name: P:Havit.Blazor.Components.Web.Bootstrap.HxPasswordStrength.InputSelector
member.summary: CSS selector of the password input to evaluate (the input option of the underlying plugin). Use when the password input cannot be placed in (the meter then attaches to the input anywhere in the document).

member.name: P:Havit.Blazor.Components.Web.Bootstrap.HxPasswordStrength.Variant
member.summary: Visualization variant of the strength meter. The default is (a four-segment meter); use for a single growing bar.

member.name: P:Havit.Blazor.Components.Web.Bootstrap.HxPasswordStrength.MinLength
member.summary: Minimum password length required for the first strength point (the minLength option of the underlying plugin). The default is 8 (underlying plugin default).

member.name: P:Havit.Blazor.Components.Web.Bootstrap.HxPasswordStrength.Weights
member.summary: Point values for the individual criteria of the scoring algorithm (the weights option of the underlying plugin). Set a weight to 0 to disable the corresponding criterion. When not set, the underlying plugin defaults are used (all criteria worth 1 point).

member.name: P:Havit.Blazor.Components.Web.Bootstrap.HxPasswordStrength.Thresholds
member.summary: Score boundaries for the strength levels (the thresholds option of the underlying plugin). When not set, the underlying plugin defaults are used (weak ≤ 2, fair ≤ 4, good ≤ 6, strong > 6).

member.name: P:Havit.Blazor.Components.Web.Bootstrap.HxPasswordStrength.ShowText
member.summary: When true, a text feedback element (strength-text) displaying the strength level message is rendered below the meter. The default is false.

member.summary: Text feedback message for the level. The default is a localized Weak.

member.summary: Text feedback message for the level. The default is a localized Fair.

member.summary: Text feedback message for the level. The default is a localized Strong.

member.summary: Strength level for a static (CSS-only) display. When set, the meter renders the level directly (filled segments, data-bs-strength attribute, text feedback) and no JavaScript evaluation takes place ( is never raised).

member.summary: Gets or sets a value indicating whether the current selection (either for single selection or for multiple selection) should be preserved during data operations, such as paging, sorting, filtering, or manual invocation of .
member.remarks: This setting ensures that the selection remains intact during operations that refresh or modify the displayed data in the grid. Note that preserving the selection requires that the underlying data items can still be matched in the updated dataset (e.g., by item1.Equals(item2)).

member.summary:
Grid to display tabular data from data source. Includes support for client-side and server-side paging & sorting (or virtualized scrolling as needed).
Full documentation and demos: https://havit.blazor.eu/components/HxGrid

member.name: M:Havit.Blazor.Components.Web.Bootstrap.HxGrid`1.GetSettings
member.summary: Provides the current settings of the grid. Override in derived classes to return a specific settings type or additional configurations.
member.remarks: Similar to , enables defining wider in components descendants (by returning a derived settings class).

member.name: P:Havit.Blazor.Components.Web.Bootstrap.HxGrid`1.DataProvider
member.summary: Data provider delegate for the grid. The data provider is responsible for fetching items to be rendered in the grid. It must always return an instance of and cannot return null.
member.remarks: The delegate is invoked to fetch data based on the current grid state, including pagination and sorting parameters.

member.name: P:Havit.Blazor.Components.Web.Bootstrap.HxGrid`1.SelectionEnabled

member.name: P:Havit.Blazor.Components.Web.Bootstrap.HxGrid`1.PreserveSelection
member.summary:
Gets or sets a value indicating whether the current selection (either for single selection or for multiple selection) should be preserved during data operations, such as paging, sorting, filtering, or manual invocation of .
Default value is false (can be set by using HxGrid.Defaults).
member.remarks: This setting ensures that the selection remains intact during operations that refresh or modify the displayed data in the grid. Note that preserving the selection requires that the underlying data items can still be matched in the updated dataset (e.g., by item1.Equals(item2)).

member.name: P:Havit.Blazor.Components.Web.Bootstrap.HxGrid`1.ContentNavigationMode
member.summary: The strategy for how data items are displayed and loaded into the grid. Supported modes include pagination, load more, and infinite scroll.

member.name: P:Havit.Blazor.Components.Web.Bootstrap.HxGrid`1.PageSize
member.summary: The number of items to display per page. Applicable for grid modes such as pagination and load more. Set to 0 to disable paging.

member.name: P:Havit.Blazor.Components.Web.Bootstrap.HxGrid`1.ShowFooterWhenEmptyData
member.summary: Determines whether the grid footer is rendered when the grid's data source is empty. The default value is false.

member.name: P:Havit.Blazor.Components.Web.Bootstrap.HxGrid`1.PagerSettings
member.summary: Pager settings.

member.name: P:Havit.Blazor.Components.Web.Bootstrap.HxGrid`1.LoadMoreButtonText
member.summary: The text for the "Load more" button, used in the navigation mode. The default text is obtained from localization resources.

member.name: P:Havit.Blazor.Components.Web.Bootstrap.HxGrid`1.LoadMoreButtonSettings
member.summary: Configuration for the "Load more" button, including appearance and behavior settings. Relevant in grid modes that use a "Load more" button for data navigation.

member.name: P:Havit.Blazor.Components.Web.Bootstrap.HxGrid`1.CurrentUserState
member.summary: Gets or sets the current state of the grid, including pagination and sorting information. This state can be used to restore the grid to a specific configuration or to synchronize it with external state management systems.

member.name: P:Havit.Blazor.Components.Web.Bootstrap.HxGrid`1.CurrentUserStateChanged
member.summary: Event that fires when the property changes. This event can be used to react to changes in the grid's state, such as sorting or pagination adjustments.

member.name: M:Havit.Blazor.Components.Web.Bootstrap.HxGrid`1.InvokeCurrentUserStateChangedAsync(Havit.Blazor.Components.Web.Bootstrap.GridUserState)
member.summary: Triggers the event. This method can be overridden in derived components to add custom logic before or after the state change event is triggered.

member.name: P:Havit.Blazor.Components.Web.Bootstrap.HxGrid`1.ProgressIndicatorDelay
member.summary: Delay in milliseconds before the progress indicator is displayed. The default value is 300 ms.

member.name: P:Havit.Blazor.Components.Web.Bootstrap.HxGrid`1.InProgress
member.summary: Gets or sets a value indicating whether the grid is currently processing data, such as loading or refreshing items. When set to null, the progress state is automatically managed based on the data provider's activity.

member.name: P:Havit.Blazor.Components.Web.Bootstrap.HxGrid`1.TableContainerCssClass
member.summary: Custom CSS class for the div element that wraps the main table element. Excludes the which is not wrapped in this div element.

member.name: P:Havit.Blazor.Components.Web.Bootstrap.HxGrid`1.TableCssClass
member.summary: Custom CSS class for the main table element of the grid. This class allows for styling and customization of the grid's appearance.

member.name: P:Havit.Blazor.Components.Web.Bootstrap.HxGrid`1.TableHeaderCssClass
member.summary: Custom CSS class for the thead element of the grid. This class allows for styling and customization of the grid's appearance.

member.name: P:Havit.Blazor.Components.Web.Bootstrap.HxGrid`1.HeaderRowCssClass
member.summary: Custom CSS class for the header tr element in the grid. Enables specific styling for the header row separate from the rest of the grid.

member.name: P:Havit.Blazor.Components.Web.Bootstrap.HxGrid`1.ItemRowCssClass
member.summary: Custom CSS class for the data tr elements in the grid. This class is applied to each row of data, providing a way to customize the styling of data rows.

member.name: P:Havit.Blazor.Components.Web.Bootstrap.HxGrid`1.ItemRowHeight
member.summary: Height of each item row, used in calculations for infinite scrolling (). The default value (41px) corresponds to the typical row height in the Bootstrap 5 default theme. The row height is not applied for other navigation modes, use CSS for that.

member.name: P:Havit.Blazor.Components.Web.Bootstrap.HxGrid`1.ItemRowCssClassSelector
member.summary: Function that defines a custom CSS class for each data tr element based on the item it represents. This allows for conditional styling of rows based on their data.

member.name: P:Havit.Blazor.Components.Web.Bootstrap.HxGrid`1.ItemKeySelector
member.summary:
Optionally defines a value for @key on each rendered row. Typically this should be used to specify a unique identifier, such as a primary key value, for each data item.
This allows the grid to preserve the association between row elements and data items based on their unique identifiers, even when the TGridItem instances are replaced by new copies (for example, after a new query against the underlying data store).
If not set, the @key will be the TItem instance itself.
member.remarks: Inspired by QuickGrid

member.name: P:Havit.Blazor.Components.Web.Bootstrap.HxGrid`1.FooterRowCssClass
member.summary: A custom CSS class for the footer tr element in the grid. This allows styling of the grid footer independently of other grid elements.

member.name: P:Havit.Blazor.Components.Web.Bootstrap.HxGrid`1.PlaceholdersRowCount
member.summary: The number of placeholder rows to be rendered in the grid. Placeholders are used when loading data or when is not set. Set to 0 to disable placeholders. Default value is 5.

member.name: P:Havit.Blazor.Components.Web.Bootstrap.HxGrid`1.OverscanCount
member.summary: Defines the number of additional items to be rendered before and after the visible region in an infinite scrolling scenario. This helps to reduce the frequency of rendering during scrolling, though higher values increase the number of elements present in the page. Default is 3.

member.name: P:Havit.Blazor.Components.Web.Bootstrap.HxGrid`1.Responsive
member.summary: Determines if the grid should be scrollable horizontally across different breakpoints. When set to true, the table-responsive class is added to the table. Default is false.

member.name: P:Havit.Blazor.Components.Web.Bootstrap.HxGrid`1.Hover
member.summary: Enables or disables the hover state on table rows within a <tbody>. When not set, the table is hoverable by default if selection is enabled. This property customizes the hover behavior of the grid rows.

member.name: P:Havit.Blazor.Components.Web.Bootstrap.HxGrid`1.Striped
member.summary: Adds zebra-striping to any table row within the <tbody> for better readability. Rows will have alternating background colors. Default is false.

member.name: P:Havit.Blazor.Components.Web.Bootstrap.HxGrid`1.SortAscendingIcon
member.summary: Icon to indicate the ascending sort direction in the column header. This icon is displayed when a column is sorted in ascending order.

member.name: P:Havit.Blazor.Components.Web.Bootstrap.HxGrid`1.SortDescendingIcon
member.summary: Icon to indicate the descending sort direction in the column header. This icon is shown when a column is sorted in descending order.

member.name: P:Havit.Blazor.Components.Web.Bootstrap.HxGrid`1.ItemRowAdditionalAttributesSelector
member.summary: Defines a function that returns additional attributes for a specific tr element based on the item it represents. This allows for custom behavior or event handling on a per-row basis.
member.remarks: If both and are specified, both dictionaries are combined into one. Note that there is no prevention of duplicate keys, which may result in a .

member.name: P:Havit.Blazor.Components.Web.Bootstrap.HxGrid`1.ItemRowAdditionalAttributes
member.summary: Provides a dictionary of additional attributes to apply to all body tr elements in the grid. These attributes can be used to customize the appearance or behavior of rows.
member.remarks: If both and are specified, both dictionaries are combined into one. Note that there is no prevention of duplicate keys, which may result in a .

member.name: P:Havit.Blazor.Components.Web.Bootstrap.HxGrid`1.HeaderRowAdditionalAttributes
member.summary: Provides a dictionary of additional attributes to apply to the header tr element of the grid. This allows for custom styling or behavior of the header row.

member.name: P:Havit.Blazor.Components.Web.Bootstrap.HxGrid`1.FooterRowAdditionalAttributes
member.summary: Provides a dictionary of additional attributes to apply to the footer tr element of the grid. This allows for custom styling or behavior of the footer row.

member.name: M:Havit.Blazor.Components.Web.Bootstrap.HxGrid`1.ItemRowAdditionalAttributesSelectorEffective(`0)
member.summary: Determines the effective additional attributes for a given data row, combining both the global and per-item attributes.
member.param: The data item for the current row.
member.returns: A dictionary of additional attributes to apply to the row.
member.exception: Thrown when there are duplicate keys in the combined dictionaries.

member.summary: Retrieves the default settings for the grid. This method can be overridden in derived classes to provide different default settings or to use a derived settings class.

member.summary: Global settings for the Havit Blazor Components library.

member.summary: Bootstrap version used by the library.

member.name: M:Havit.Blazor.Components.Web.Bootstrap.HxSetup.RenderBootstrapJavaScriptReference
member.summary:
Renders the <script> tag that references the corresponding Bootstrap JavaScript bundle (with Floating UI).
To be used in _Layout.cshtml as @Html.Raw(HxSetup.RenderBootstrapJavaScriptReference()).
member.remarks:
Bootstrap 6 ships ES modules only — there is no UMD bundle and no implicit window.bootstrap global anymore. The bundle is shipped with this library (static web asset) to stay in lock-step with the compiled CSS (Bootstrap 6 alpha is not available on public CDNs yet). The rendered module script re-exposes window.bootstrap as a compatibility bridge for the Hx* JS modules (to be replaced by explicit imports as components are migrated to v6). Note: the library's JS initializer (Havit.Blazor.Components.Web.Bootstrap.lib.module.js, beforeWebStart/beforeStart) also establishes this bridge before the Blazor runtime starts, which guarantees availability for component JS interop regardless of module-script timing; this reference remains recommended so the bundle download starts as early as possible.
We do not want to use TagHelper or HTML Helper here as we do not want to introduce a dependency on server-side ASP.NET Core (MVC/Razor) to our library (a separate NuGet package would have to be created).

member.name: M:Havit.Blazor.Components.Web.Bootstrap.HxSetup.RenderBootstrapCssReference(Havit.Blazor.Components.Web.Bootstrap.BootstrapFlavor)
member.summary:
Renders the <link> tag that references the corresponding Bootstrap CSS.
To be used in _Layout.cshtml as @Html.Raw(HxSetup.RenderBootstrapCssReference()).
member.remarks: We do not want to use TagHelper or HTML Helper here as we do not want to introduce a dependency on server-side ASP.NET Core (MVC/Razor) to our library (a separate NuGet package would have to be created).

member.name: T:Havit.Blazor.Components.Web.Bootstrap.HxListLayout`1
member.summary:
Provides a unified layout for data presentation components and associated filtering controls.
This component orchestrates the interaction between filter controls and the data presentation component. The data list is typically implemented using a component. Filters are displayed in a component, while filter values are shown as . Additionally, it supports predefined named views for quick switching between different filter configurations and other features such as a title, search box, and commands. Full documentation and demos: https://havit.blazor.eu/components/HxListLayout

member.name: M:Havit.Blazor.Components.Web.Bootstrap.HxListLayout`1.GetDefaults
member.summary: Returns the defaults. Enables overriding defaults in descendants (use a separate set of defaults).

member.summary: Set of settings to be applied to the component instance (overrides , overridden by individual parameters).

member.summary: Returns an optional set of component settings.
member.remarks: Similar to , enables defining wider in component descendants (by returning a derived settings class).

member.summary: Title of the component. If is true and is not null, the component's title displays the name of the currently selected Named View.

member.name: P:Havit.Blazor.Components.Web.Bootstrap.HxListLayout`1.TitleTemplate
member.summary: Title of the component (in form of RenderFragment). If is true and is not null, the component's title displays the name of the currently selected Named View.

member.name: P:Havit.Blazor.Components.Web.Bootstrap.HxListLayout`1.NamedViews
member.summary: Represents the collection of Named Views available for selection. Each Named View defines a pre-set filter configuration that can be applied to the data.
member.remarks: Named Views provide a convenient way for users to quickly apply commonly used filters to the data set. Ensure that each Named View in the collection has a unique name which accurately describes its filter criteria.

member.name: P:Havit.Blazor.Components.Web.Bootstrap.HxListLayout`1.SelectedNamedView
member.summary: Selected named view (highlighted in the list with .active CSS class).

member.summary: Triggers the event. Allows interception of the event in derived components.

member.name: P:Havit.Blazor.Components.Web.Bootstrap.HxListLayout`1.TitleFromNamedView
member.summary: Indicates whether the name of the selected Named View () is automatically used as title. If true, the component's title changes to match the name of the currently selected Named View. Useful for dynamic title updates based on user selections from predefined views. The default value is true.
member.remarks: This update occurs upon the selection of a new Named View. It allows the Title to reflect the current data filtering context provided by the Named Views, enhancing user understanding of the active filter.

member.summary:
Bootstrap 6 Menu component (replaces the Bootstrap 5 Dropdown).
Renders the and the .menu element (with ) as direct siblings — Bootstrap 6 requires no wrapper element (sibling position is also used by the CSS, e.g. for split-button border radius via :has(+ .menu)).

You can use the AutoClose parameter to change this behavior of the menu. https://v6-dev--twbs-bootstrap.netlify.app/docs/6.0/components/menu/#auto-close-behavior. The parameter can be used to override the settings of the component or to specify the auto-close behavior when the component is not used.

member.name: T:Havit.Blazor.Components.Web.Bootstrap.AnchorFragmentNavigationAutomationMode
member.summary: Automation mode for .

member.name: F:Havit.Blazor.Components.Web.Bootstrap.AnchorFragmentNavigationAutomationMode.Full
member.summary: Scrolls to the anchor on firstRender and whenever the location changes (). With , this mode is suitable only for static pages (where the size/offset of individual sections remains the same from the very beginning). Use or when the page contents load asynchronously and the layout changes.

member.name: F:Havit.Blazor.Components.Web.Bootstrap.AnchorFragmentNavigationAutomationMode.Manual
member.summary: Explicit calls to or are needed. Use this mode with when you need scrollspy to call to recalculate the target offsets before scrolling (scrollspy does not work properly on scrolled content).

member.name: F:Havit.Blazor.Components.Web.Bootstrap.AnchorFragmentNavigationAutomationMode.SamePage
member.summary: Same as , but scrolls to the anchor on if the page remains the same (just the #fragment portion changed). Works for most scenarios when you refresh the after data load and then you just need to navigate over the page.

member.name: T:Havit.Blazor.Components.Web.Bootstrap.HxAnchorFragmentNavigation
member.summary:
Temporarily (?) compensates for a Blazor deficiency in anchor fragments (scrolling to page#id URLs). Supports navigation with the component. GitHub Issue: Blazor 0.8.0: hash routing to named element #8393.
Full documentation and demos: https://havit.blazor.eu/components/HxAnchorFragmentNavigation

member.name: P:Havit.Blazor.Components.Web.Bootstrap.HxAnchorFragmentNavigation.Automation
member.summary: The level of automation. The default is .

member.name: T:Havit.Blazor.Components.Web.Bootstrap.HxNavbarDrawer
member.summary: Container for the responsive content of the navbar. Bootstrap 6 replaced the v5 .navbar-collapse (Collapse plugin) with a Drawer: below the navbar's expand breakpoint the content opens as a drawer (toggled by via the Bootstrap data API), at or above the breakpoint the drawer markup is flattened into inline navbar content by the Bootstrap CSS.

member.summary: Title rendered in the drawer header (visible only below the navbar's expand breakpoint).

member.summary: Placement of the drawer when opened below the expand breakpoint. The default is .

member.summary: Bootstrap navbar-toggler component. The toggler icon is rendered via a CSS mask-image tinted with currentcolor (no separate light/dark icons needed in Bootstrap 6). In Bootstrap 6 the toggler opens the navbar's responsive content as a Drawer (see ) via the Bootstrap data API — the v5 Collapse plugin is no longer used for navbars.

member.name: T:Havit.Blazor.Components.Web.Bootstrap.HxNavOverflow
member.summary:
Bootstrap Nav overflow component (new in Bootstrap 6, "Priority+" pattern).
Automatically moves nav items which do not fit the available width into a "More" overflow menu. The overflow is responsive to the container width (uses a ResizeObserver), not the viewport width.
Unlike the Bootstrap NavOverflow plugin (which clones nav items into the menu and is therefore incompatible with Blazor rendering), the component renders the nav content twice (once in the nav, once in the overflow menu) and only toggles the visibility of the individual items. All items remain fully Blazor-owned (re-rendering, event handlers, and active state work in both places).
Full documentation and demos: https://havit.blazor.eu/components/HxNavOverflow

member.name: F:Havit.Blazor.Components.Web.Bootstrap.HxNavOverflow.KeepVisibleCssClass
member.summary: CSS class which prevents an item from being moved to the overflow menu (Bootstrap nav-overflow-keep). Apply to the nav item, e.g. <HxNavLink CssClass="@HxNavOverflow.KeepVisibleCssClass" />.

member.name: P:Havit.Blazor.Components.Web.Bootstrap.HxNavOverflow.Defaults
member.summary: Application-wide defaults for and derived components.

member.name: M:Havit.Blazor.Components.Web.Bootstrap.HxNavOverflow.GetDefaults
member.summary: Returns application-wide defaults for the component. Enables overriding defaults in descendants (use a separate set of defaults).

member.name: P:Havit.Blazor.Components.Web.Bootstrap.HxNavOverflow.Settings
member.summary: Set of settings to be applied to the component instance (overrides , overridden by individual parameters).

member.name: M:Havit.Blazor.Components.Web.Bootstrap.HxNavOverflow.GetSettings
member.summary: Returns an optional set of component settings.
member.remarks: Similar to , enables defining wider in component descendants (by returning a derived settings class).

member.name: P:Havit.Blazor.Components.Web.Bootstrap.HxNavOverflow.Id
member.summary: ID of the nav element.

member.summary: The visual variant of the nav items. The default value is .

member.summary: Text of the "More" toggle button. The default is More. Set to null or an empty string to render an icon-only toggle.

member.summary: Icon of the "More" toggle button. The default is .

member.summary: Position of the icon relative to the text in the "More" toggle button. The default is .

member.summary: Placement of the overflow menu relative to the "More" toggle button. The default is .

member.name: P:Havit.Blazor.Components.Web.Bootstrap.HxNavOverflow.MinimumVisibleItems
member.summary: Minimum number of items to keep visible before the remaining items overflow into the menu (mirrors the Bootstrap threshold option). The default is 0.

member.name: P:Havit.Blazor.Components.Web.Bootstrap.HxNavOverflow.CollapseBelow
member.summary: Container width threshold below which all items collapse into the overflow menu (mirrors the Bootstrap collapseBelow option). A breakpoint name (e.g. md, resolved from the --bs-breakpoint-{name} CSS variable) or a pixel value (e.g. 768). Items with the CSS class remain visible. The default is null (disabled).

member.name: P:Havit.Blazor.Components.Web.Bootstrap.HxNavOverflow.OnOverflowChanged
member.summary: Raised when the number of items in the overflow menu changes (including back to zero).

member.summary: Triggers the event. Allows interception of the event in derived components.

member.name: P:Havit.Blazor.Components.Web.Bootstrap.HxNavOverflow.CssClass
member.summary: Additional CSS class.

member.name: P:Havit.Blazor.Components.Web.Bootstrap.HxNavOverflow.ChildContent
member.summary: Content of the nav (typically components). The content is rendered twice—once as the nav items and once as the overflow menu items (visibility of the individual items is toggled based on the available width).

member.name: P:Havit.Blazor.Components.Web.Bootstrap.HxNavOverflow.AdditionalAttributes
member.summary: Additional attributes to be splatted onto the underlying <nav> element.

member.name: M:Havit.Blazor.Components.Web.Bootstrap.HxNavOverflow.UpdateAsync
member.summary: Recalculates which items should overflow. Called automatically on container resize and on nav content changes, use only when the automatic detection is not sufficient.

member.name: M:Havit.Blazor.Components.Web.Bootstrap.HxNavOverflow.HandleOverflowChanged(System.Int32,System.Int32)
member.summary: Receives a notification from JavaScript when the overflow changes.

member.name: T:Havit.Blazor.Components.Web.Bootstrap.HxStepper
member.summary:
Bootstrap Stepper component (new in Bootstrap 6).
Displays progress through a multi-step workflow (wizards, timelines, step-by-step progress bars) as a sequence of numbered steps (s). The component is CSS-only, there is no step-switching logic (wire up your own state).
Full documentation and demos: https://havit.blazor.eu/components/HxStepper

member.summary: Changes the layout of the stepper items from vertical to horizontal, including the responsive breakpoint (Bootstrap 6 responsive {breakpoint}:stepper-horizontal classes are container query based, the component renders the required contains-inline wrapper automatically). The default is (vertical stepper).

member.summary: Theme color of the stepper (renders the theme-* class), applies to all active items. To color individual steps, use .

member.summary: When true, the stepper is wrapped in a stepper-overflow container which enables horizontal scrolling when the (horizontal) stepper overflows its parent. The default is false.

member.summary: Additional attributes to be splatted onto an underlying HTML element (e.g. use the style attribute to customize the gap by overriding the --bs-stepper-gap CSS variable).

member.name: P:Havit.Blazor.Components.Web.Bootstrap.HxStepper.RenderContainsInlineWrapper
member.summary: Responsive horizontal steppers use container queries, which require a contains-inline parent element (when the stepper-overflow wrapper is rendered, it establishes the query container itself).

member.name: T:Havit.Blazor.Components.Web.Bootstrap.HxPopover
member.summary:
Bootstrap Popover component.
Rendered as a span wrapper to fully support popovers on disabled elements (see example in Disabled elements in the Bootstrap popover documentation).
Full documentation and demos: https://havit.blazor.eu/components/HxPopover

member.name: P:Havit.Blazor.Components.Web.Bootstrap.HxPopover.Defaults
member.summary: Application-wide defaults for the and derived components.

member.name: M:Havit.Blazor.Components.Web.Bootstrap.HxPopover.GetDefaults
member.summary: Returns application-wide defaults for the component. Enables overriding defaults in descendants (use a separate set of defaults).

member.name: P:Havit.Blazor.Components.Web.Bootstrap.HxPopover.Settings
member.summary: Set of settings to be applied to the component instance (overrides , overridden by individual parameters).

member.name: M:Havit.Blazor.Components.Web.Bootstrap.HxPopover.GetSettings
member.summary: Returns an optional set of component settings.
member.remarks: Similar to , enables defining wider in component descendants (by returning a derived settings class).

member.name: P:Havit.Blazor.Components.Web.Bootstrap.HxPopover.Title
member.summary: Popover title.

member.name: P:Havit.Blazor.Components.Web.Bootstrap.HxPopover.Content
member.summary: Popover content.

member.name: P:Havit.Blazor.Components.Web.Bootstrap.HxPopover.Placement
member.summary: Popover placement. The default is "not set" (which Bootstrap defaults to right).

member.name: P:Havit.Blazor.Components.Web.Bootstrap.HxPopover.Trigger
member.summary: Popover trigger(s). The default is "not set" (which Bootstrap defaults to click).

member.name: T:Havit.Blazor.Components.Web.Bootstrap.HxTooltip
member.summary:
Bootstrap Tooltip component, activates on hover.
Rendered as a span wrapper to fully support tooltips on disabled elements (see example in Disabled elements in the Bootstrap tooltip documentation).
Full documentation and demos: https://havit.blazor.eu/components/HxTooltip

member.name: P:Havit.Blazor.Components.Web.Bootstrap.HxTooltip.Defaults
member.summary: Application-wide defaults for the and derived components.

member.name: M:Havit.Blazor.Components.Web.Bootstrap.HxTooltip.GetDefaults
member.summary: Returns application-wide defaults for the component. Enables overriding defaults in descendants (use a separate set of defaults).

member.name: P:Havit.Blazor.Components.Web.Bootstrap.HxTooltip.Settings
member.summary: Set of settings to be applied to the component instance (overrides , overridden by individual parameters).

member.name: M:Havit.Blazor.Components.Web.Bootstrap.HxTooltip.GetSettings
member.summary: Returns an optional set of component settings.
member.remarks: Similar to , enables defining wider in component descendants (by returning a derived settings class).

member.name: P:Havit.Blazor.Components.Web.Bootstrap.HxTooltip.Text
member.summary: Tooltip text.

member.name: P:Havit.Blazor.Components.Web.Bootstrap.HxTooltip.Placement
member.summary: Tooltip placement. The default is "not set" (which Bootstrap defaults to top).

member.name: P:Havit.Blazor.Components.Web.Bootstrap.HxTooltip.Trigger
member.summary: Tooltip trigger(s). The default is "not set" (which Bootstrap defaults to hover + focus).
